title: Latin Corn and Rice Flour Pancakes

ingredients.name: fresh or frozen sweet corn kernels

subs.name: besan (gram flour) for a nuttier, more savoury pancake

ingredients.name: egg
visual_checks: adds binding; skip for eggless version
ingredients.notes: adds binding; skip for eggless version

subs.name: 1 tbsp extra rice flour and 1 tsp baking powder for a fully eggless binder
white or brown sugar — the flavour of jaggery is preferred for its caramel note
coconut oil adds a tropical flavour that suits the corn very well

steps.text: Set aside one-third of the corn kernels. Blend the remaining two-thirds with the milk, egg (if using), jaggery and salt in a blender until you get a thick, slightly grainy batter — do not over-blend, some texture is ideal.

Pour the blended batter into a bowl and fold in the reserved whole corn kernels, rice flour, and green chilli and coriander if using. The batter should be thick enough to hold its shape when dropped from a spoon — add a splash more milk if it seems too stiff.

Heat a tawa over medium flame and add a small knob of butter or a drizzle of oil. Spoon 3–4 tbsp of batter per pancake onto the tawa, spreading gently into a circle about 8 cm across. Cook for 3 minutes until the edges set and the underside is golden.

Flip carefully and cook the second side for 2–3 minutes until golden and cooked through — press lightly with a spatula; no wet batter should ooze from the sides. Repeat with remaining batter, adding butter to the tawa between batches.
Serve the pancakes warm, either sweet — with a drizzle of honey or a small knob of butter — or savoury with thick curd, pico de gallo or any chutney. The pancakes are best eaten fresh off the tawa.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: egg → 1 tbsp extra rice flour and 1 tsp baking powder for a fully eggless binder (texture will be slightly denser); rice-flour → besan (gram flour) for a nuttier, more savoury pancake; jaggery → white or brown sugar — the flavour of jaggery is preferred for its caramel note; butter → coconut oil adds a tropical flavour that suits the corn very well
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Sweet corn is widely available year-round in India at vegetable markets and frozen in supermarkets. Rice flour (arisi maavu) is a South Indian pantry staple. This recipe bridges Mexican and Indian tiffin traditions — the pancake technique is identical to making a thick dosa. Jaggery (vellam) adds the authentic sweet note. A mixie does the blending job perfectly.
